current: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c with r_mu_n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu_n r_e_n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2: the clipped-AIPW ERM welfare regret is bounded by the GLOBAL (additive, NON-localized) estimated-nuisance drift r_mu_n r_e_n/q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} plus a localized stochastic term whose radius uses the AIPW conditional VARIANCE Var(Gamma_q|X) <~ 1/max{p,q} (scale n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2}), NOT the crude envelope q^{-1}. Balancing the clip q=n^{-s} gives g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma), g(a,c,gamma)}. At gamma=0 (strict-overlap floor, q fixed) r_feas=min{A_alpha, c}, which equals r_star=A_alpha when c>=A_alpha. The drift is GLOBAL because ||b_q||_2 cannot carry the r_mu r_e product from L2 bounds on each factor; only E|b_q| (Cauchy-Schwarz, L1) gives the product, and that enters regret additively, not through the margin self-bound.
proposed: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and polynomial nuisance exponents a,c with r_mu_n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu_n r_e_n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, define g(a,c,gamma)=min{A_alpha, c, A_alpha(1+2 gamma a)/(1+2 gamma A_alpha), (c+2 gamma a)/(1+2 gamma)}. Equivalently g=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}. Let s_*=max{0,2 gamma(g-a)} and choose q_n=min{1/4,n^{-s_*}}. Define r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, choose any fixed q<underline_p and set r_feas(alpha,0,a,c)=min{A_alpha,c}, which equals r_star(alpha,0)=A_alpha exactly when c>=A_alpha.
reason: The open-ended feasible-upper node asks for the derived clip schedule and closed-form exponent. The frozen definition already has the correct additive-drift optimization; this correction evaluates the one-dimensional balance explicitly and records the optimizer q_n.
